input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to show them how to set up AppShell.xaml for a .NET MAUI app with:
1. A flyout menu with two sections:
   - "Dashboard" (single page)
   - "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and "Archived")
2. A Settings page accessible from the flyout
3. Route registration for a ProductDetailsPage

I'll create an example AppShell.xaml and the corresponding AppShell.xaml.cs with route registration.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:views="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Views"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout"
    Title="MyMauiApp">

    <!-- Dashboard: single page in its own flyout section -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Dashboard"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:DashboardPage}"
            Route="dashboard" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products: two tabs (Active / Archived) under one flyout entry -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="products.png">
        <Tab Title="Products">
            <ShellContent
                Title="Active"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ActiveProductsPage}"
                Route="active" />
            <ShellContent
                Title="Archived"
                ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:ArchivedProductsPage}"
                Route="archived" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings: single page in its own flyout section -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="settings.png">
        <ShellContent
            Title="Settings"
            ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate views:SettingsPage}"
            Route="settings" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>

</Shell>

data.content:
Here's the setup:

**AppShell.xaml** – defines the flyout structure:

- **`FlyoutItem "Dashboard"`** – single `ShellContent`, renders as one page.
- **`FlyoutItem "Products"`** – wraps a `<Tab>` with two `ShellContent` entries ("Active" / "Archived"), which appear as bottom tabs when that flyout item is selected.
- **`FlyoutItem "Settings"`** – another single page.

**AppShell.xaml.cs** – registers the detail route:

```csharp
Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(Views.ProductDetailsPage), typeof(Views.ProductDetailsPage));
```

**Navigating to details** from a product list:

```csharp
await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
```

To receive the parameter, add `[QueryProperty("Id", "id")]` to `ProductDetailsPage` (or use `IQueryAttributable`).

The files are at `/tmp/sv-d0309a07f9b24eb6b130def2f71558b9/maui-example/` if you'd like to copy them into your project.
